self_improvement.claim: Directive Workspace can improve Engine planning quality by making proof planning consume typed extraction, adaptation, and improvement output instead of flattening that stage onto the same base planning input.
self_improvement.mechanism: This DEEP bounded result turns the already-landed extraction-to-adaptation-to-improvement seam into a third bounded Architecture implementation target for proof-stage chaining.
self_improvement.baseline_observation: Before this DEEP case, the Engine proof plan still used only planningInput even though extraction, adaptation, and improvement already produced typed output inside processSource().
self_improvement.expected_effect: The Engine can now carry forward earlier stage boundaries into proof planning without touching integration planning in the same slice.

decision.rationale: The current Engine shape justifies one bounded proof-stage chaining slice as the next product-owned Architecture implementation target.
decision.runtime_threshold_check: yes - the mechanism is still valuable without a runtime surface, so Architecture should retain product-owned value
